title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses the historical and social role of forgiveness, potentially exploring how it functioned as a constraint or mechanism in past societies. This is a philosophical or historical commentary piece.
writeup: Ada Palmer examines the historical and social role of forgiveness, framing it not as a simple virtue but as a constraint or mechanism that operated within past societies. The discussion is philosophical and historical commentary, considering how the expectation or practice of forgiveness could function as a trap rather than a release. The central takeaway is that forgiveness carried structural consequences in earlier social orders, shaping behavior beyond personal reconciliation.
